channel.title: NYT > Health
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Rich Danker, RFK Jr.’s Top Spokesman, Resigns in Protest Over New Vaping Policy
item.description: In a letter to President Trump, the spokesman, Rich Danker, said allowing the sale of flavored e-cigarettes would enhance their appeal to children.
item.dc:creator: Sheryl Gay Stolberg
item.media:credit: Michael A. McCoy for The New York Times
item.media:description: The resignation comes at a fraught time for the Department of Health and Human Services, which now has three major vacancies.

item.title: With a Friend in Trump, the Tobacco Industry Secures a Lucrative Win
item.description: In a dispute over vapes, the president sided with tobacco companies that filled his groups’ coffers over his own F.D.A. commissioner, who resigned in protest.
item.dc:creator: Christina Jewett and Kenneth P. Vogel
item.media:description: The F.D.A. issued new guidance that could pave the way for major tobacco companies to begin selling flavored vapes less than a week after industry executives met with President Trump.

item.title: Nancy Cox, Who Worked to Conquer the Wily Flu, Dies at 77
item.description: As the leader of the C.D.C.’s influenza division, she battled to keep up with an ever-changing viral opponent, building a global network of researchers and forecasters.
item.dc:creator: Clay Risen
item.category: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention

item.title: Marty Makary, Trump’s F.D.A. Commissioner, Resigns After Weeks of Pressure
item.description: The agency’s top food official will step in to the role after Dr. Makary privately said he opposed the administration decision to approve flavored vapes.
item.dc:creator: Christina Jewett
item.media:credit: Al Drago for The New York Times
item.media:description: Dr. Marty Makary at times tried to advance his own principles alongside the administration’s goals, which at times were at odds with the powerful food, tobacco and pharmaceutical industries.

item.title: How Worried Should We Be About Hantavirus?
item.description: Our global health reporter, Apoorva Mandavilli, explains that there is no indication the hantavirus outbreak could turn into a pandemic — but also that the public might not be getting all of the details.
item.dc:creator: Apoorva Mandavilli, Nikolay Nikolov, Léo Hamelin, Sutton Raphael, Rafaela Balster and Stephanie Swart

item.title: How an ‘Impossible’ Idea Led to a Pancreatic Cancer Breakthrough
item.description: The new strategy also holds promise for lung and colon tumors. Here’s how scientists discovered it.
item.dc:creator: Gina Kolata and Rebecca Robbins
item.media:credit: Sophie Park for The New York Times

item.title: Why So Many Guys Are Obsessed With Testosterone
item.description: From the Trump administration to online influencers, the hormone is increasingly seen as the key to achieving a new male ideal.
item.dc:creator: Azeen Ghorayshi

item.title: RFK Jr. Is Driving a Vast Inquiry Into Vaccines, Despite His Public Silence
item.description: Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. has toned down his public criticism of vaccines, under orders from the White House. But inside his department, a sprawling research effort is a top priority.
item.dc:creator: Christina Jewett and Sheryl Gay Stolberg
item.category: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
item.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times

item.title: A Single Infusion Could Suppress H.I.V. for Years, Study Suggests
item.description: A study of a few patients, to be presented this week, showed promise for a type of therapy that has already cured some blood cancers.
item.dc:creator: Apoorva Mandavilli
item.media:description: A study led by an H.I.V. expert at the University of California, San Francisco, could offer a path toward a lasting H.I.V. treatment.

item.title: Supreme Court Continues Access to Abortion Pill by Mail, for a Few Days
item.description: Justice Samuel A. Alito Jr. has paused until at least Thursday a federal appeals court ruling against the Food and Drug Administration that would restrict access by mail to mifepristone.
item.dc:creator: Ann E. Marimow
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
item.media:description: The justices acted in response to a request from two mifepristone manufacturers that asked the Supreme Court to intervene this month after the conservative U.S. Court of Appeals for the Fifth Circuit restricted access to the pills.

item.title: Hantavirus Vaccines and Treatments Are in the Pipeline
item.description: But it has been hard to attract interest in medical interventions for viruses that have not been considered a top public health priority, scientists say.
item.dc:creator: Emily Anthes
item.media:description: An ambulance boat approaching the cruise ship MV Hondius, which has sustained a deadly hantavirus outbreak, off Cape Verde on Tuesday.

item.title: With Commissioner Under Pressure, F.D.A. Opens Door to Flavored Vapes
item.description: Though illicit e-cigarettes have flooded in from China, the new policy could allow major tobacco companies to sell from prime shelf space at thousands of stores.
item.dc:creator: Christina Jewett
item.media:credit: Justin J Wee for The New York Times
item.media:description: Rates of adolescent vaping have fallen steadily in recent years.

item.title: Cruise Ship Linked to Hantavirus Outbreak Arrives in Spain’s Canary Islands for Disembarking
item.description: Small groups of passengers and crew left the ship after it anchored off Spain’s Canary Islands on Sunday. Those aboard were expected to be flown to their home countries, officials said.
item.dc:creator: Carlos Barragán, Amelia Nierenberg and Lynsey Chutel
item.media:description: A passenger from the MV Hondius speaking with medical staff in the Granadilla Port in Tenerife, Canary Islands, on Sunday.

item.title: Trump Plans to Fire F.D.A. Commissioner Marty Makary
item.description: Dr. Makary has been a supporter of the Make American Healthy Again Movement but made some enemies in the administration over vaping, the abortion pill and rejections of new drugs.
item.dc:creator: Christina Jewett and Maggie Haberman
item.media:description: Dr. Marty Makary, commissioner of the Food and Drug Administration, at the White House last year.

item.title: Trump Administration Will Withhold $1.3 Billion in Medicaid Payments to California
item.description: Vice President JD Vance said the state had not done enough to fight fraud in the public health insurance program.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Kliff
item.category: Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services
item.media:description: Vice President JD Vance said that California had failed to combat fraud in Medicaid.

item.title: ‘Trimester Zero’: What to Expect When You’re Expecting to Expect
item.description: Influencers and health gurus are offering pregnancy solutions during “trimester zero,” when women are trying to conceive.
item.dc:creator: Kim Hew-Low

item.title: Cruise Ship Hit by Hantavirus Leaves Canary Islands and Sails Toward Netherlands
item.description: The last evacuees disembarked in the Canary Islands, and a smaller crew is now en route to Rotterdam, where the ship will be disinfected, officials said.
item.dc:creator: Adeel Hassan
item.media:description: Three people who were aboard the MV Hondius have died since it left from Argentina in April.

item.title: Passengers on Hantavirus Cruise Ship Will Disembark in Tenerife and Return to Home Countries
item.description: The cruise ship will arrive on the island of Tenerife, part of the Canary Islands of Spain, officials said. All of the passengers will then be evacuated to their home countries.
item.dc:creator: John S.W. MacDonald and Carlos Barragán
item.category: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
item.media:description: Workers on the island of Tenerife setting up tents at an expected reception point for passengers from the cruise ship MV Hondius.

item.title: There Are Ants in This Canadian Hospital. Again.
item.description: A hospital in Manitoba had to postpone a “limited number of elective surgeries” after ants appeared there for the third time since 2024.
item.dc:creator: Sopan Deb
item.media:description: The provincial authority that runs Carman Memorial Hospital in Carman, Manitoba, said it was working with patients to reschedule elective surgeries after ants appeared in the operating room.

item.title: Hantavirus Is Nothing Like Coronavirus, but It’s Bringing Some ‘Covid P.T.S.D.’
item.description: Experts have been quick to reassure the public after the deaths aboard the Dutch cruise ship MV Hondius, but images and turns of phrase have rekindled anxieties from Covid’s early days.
item.dc:creator: Neil Vigdor
item.media:description: Health workers in protective gear evacuating patients from the MV Hondius cruise ship yesterday.

item.title: What Is Body Dysmorphic Disorder?
item.description: It may look like vanity, but it’s a debilitating mental health condition.
item.dc:creator: Christina Caron

item.title: Hantavirus Response Shows How Trump Cuts Have Compromised U.S. Preparedness
item.description: The Trump administration has slashed funding for infectious disease research and has far fewer employees, including disease detectives, to respond to outbreaks.
item.dc:creator: Apoorva Mandavilli
item.category: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
item.media:description: An ambulance crew awaiting an evacuated passenger with a suspected hantavirus infection in Amsterdam on Wednesday. The U.S. government has released very little information about the outbreak on a cruise ship, which has affected several Americans.

item.title: Employees With Medical Conditions Challenge C.D.C. In-Office Requirement
item.description: The health agency has been particularly strict in abolishing at-home work, overriding accommodations that were granted years before the pandemic.
item.dc:creator: Apoorva Mandavilli
item.category: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
item.media:credit: Salwan Georges for The New York Times
item.media:description: Katie Schenk, an infectious disease epidemiologist, was hired as a remote C.D.C. employee in 2024 and lost her telework accommodation. After trying in vain to get it back she quit the agency in frustration in February.
